title: Stizophyllum riparium
intro: Stizophyllum riparium, also known as Ipeca, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stizophyllum riparium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stizophyllum riparium is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Stizophyllum riparium belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions and is an evergreen climber. It is a member of the genus Stizophyllum and is classified as a species of flowering plant. Stizophyllum riparium is further classified under the subfamily Rauvoloideae, which includes other species with similar characteristics. The classification of Stizophyllum riparium is consistent with the APG IV system of plant classification.
